WILLIAM I. GOSNELL, attor ney, 1151 Myrtle Avenue: "I will most likely get caught in the deadline rush. And as broke as I am, my pay- ments will cer- tainly be made in install- ments." . .

title: Rev. Hyason Diggs Now Army Chaplain
full_text: Rev. Hyason Diggs Now Army Chaplain BALTIMORE The Rev. Hyason Diggs, asso- ciate pastor of Union Baptist Del. State Girls to Church, has been commissioned a second lieutenant and made a Get War Training REV. HYASON DIGGS chaplain in the U.S. Army, it was announced this week. He will leave for Harvard Uni- versity March 5 for a period of orientation and indoctrination, after which time he will be sent to a permanent post. Chaplain Diggs, a member of the local Baptist Ministers' Con- ference and of the National Bap- tist Convention, Inc., did his col- lege and seminary work at Vir- ginia Union University. He is a member of Kappa Alpha Psi fraternity and was formerly cottage master and chaplain at Cheltenham School for Boys. DOVER, Del.-Courses in en- gineering, science and manage- ment war training, under the U.S. Office of Education, have been started at Delaware State College. The courses in chemistry, super- vised by Dr. Robert S. Beale, di- rector of the division on natural sciences and mathematics, will train college girls as laboratory technicians in chemistry for war industries. University of Delaware faculty members will conduct the courses on the campus and a large number of girls have already been en- rolled. Reproduced with permission of the copyright owner. Further reproduction prohibited without permission.

title: Monumental News
full_text: Monumental News WEEK'S NEWS IN REVIEW Clip and Send to a Service Man BALTIMORE. The board of estimates has approved the ex- penditure of $20,000 for the pur- chase of the property at 732 W. Lexington Street for a recreation center Incensed over the new system of assigning sanitary facilities on a segregated basis at the Western! Electric plants, employees threat- ened to quit their jobs. The threats highlighted a meet- ing sponsored bythe Non-Partisan Committee of Western Electric Em- ployees, who will sponsor a mass meeting at Enon Baptist Church, May 25, to protest against the segregated set-up. Colored officers who assisted Lieutenant Emerson's vice squad in three week-end raids in which fifty-one persons were hailed into Northwestern police station, were: Officers Joseph Duggins and Den- nis Mello and Auxiliary Police- men Charles Brunes and Arthur Caldwell. Cheltenham Proposal Hit The local Urban League has ex- pressed disapproval of the plan to renovate the Cheltenham School for Boys rather than to con- struct a new institution as au- thorized in a letter to Gov. Herb- ert R. O'Conor. No colored military police have yet been assigned to the Pennsyl- vania Avenue area as promised by Major-General Philip Hayes, com- manding general of the Third Service Command. The annual meeting of the Mary- land State Conference of NAACP Branches will be held May 27 at Union Baptist Church, 1219 Druid Hill Avenue. In a tribute to mothers of serv- ice men, Mrs. Fannie Pinkney of 2123 Madison Avenue was hon- ored as the ideal mother at the Gold Street USO. Whites Like "Negro Soldier" The film, "The Negro Soldier," shown concurrently at two local white theatres, received favorable responses, cheers and spasmodic bursts of handclapping. The Federal Court decision which upheld the policy of the Enoch Pratt Free Library in bar- ring colored women from its train- ing classes was appealed on May 10 by the NAACP through its at- torneys, W. A. C. Hughes and Charles H. Houston. Close Tin Factory School The board of school commis- joners approved a recommenda- ion to discontinue use of the 20- rear-old "tin factory" building at Calhoun and Gold Streets which 10w houses School No. 109. Mrs. Annie M. Jones, 4682 Falls Road, was cited by Mayor Theo- lore McKeldin for caring for over Forty children from the Henry Watson Children's Society in a hirty-year period. Miss Frances L. Murphy, di- rector, has announced that Bal. imore, Washington, Philadelphia, and Newark, N.J., have signed up to take part in the tenth annual AFRO Clean Block Campaign which begins officiallly July 1. A capacity crowd witnessed the dedication of the George F. Bragg Memorial Altar and the confirma- tion of twenty persons at St. James PE Church, Sunday, with Bishop Noble C. Powell officiating. Seventy-five Delta Sigma Theta sorors attended the Eastern Re- gional Conference, Saturday, at Grace Presbyterian Church. 7 Scout Leaders Named Local Boy Scout officials have announced that seven recently ap- pointed neighborhood commission- ers will serve in the Chesapeake District. They are: Leon Bailey, Joel Bouldin, AlexGray, Frank Mon- roe, James C. Robinson, Walter Stewart and James S. Williams. Aaron Lancey will serve in the Catonsville area. Tried at Western police court, Tuesday afternoon, Walter Mc- Gowan, 19, of 777 W. Pratt Street, was sentenced to three months in jail and fined $25 and costs for an alleged attack on a white ex- soldier in Southwest Baltimore. 8 Get Park Police Jobs Four men and four women added to the city park police force are David Solomon, Richard R. Brown, Albert J. Hardy, Paul R. Loving: Miss Minnie Arrington and Mesdames Gladys W. Davis, Cora L. Lewis and Gertrude Bush. The Rev. Reginald J. Daniels, 27, of Dayton, Ohio, was elected pas- tor of Madison Street Presbyterian Church to succeed the Rev. W. W. Walker who retired in 1942 after forty-two years' service. Won: The Douglass High School track team defeated Carver and Vocational in triangular meet. Douglass, 88 points; Carver, 31, and Dunbar, 30. ELOPED: Miss Anethia Barks- dale, daughter of Dr. and Mrs. M. L. Barksdale, and Roland B. Williams to Westminster. Md. WED: Mrs. Victoria Gray and Charles Sherwood, and William Fennell, 76, to Mrs. Luvanie Wil liams, 67. DEAD: Charles H. Lynch, 60 Social Security Board employee. BORN: A daughter, to Capt and Mrs. Leroy Clay (Monteres Kenny): the Tazwell Lewise! (Elizabeth Harris); a son, the Ver non Hutchinses, and Cpl. and Mrs Edward Williams (Zerita Lewis) Reproduced with permission of the copyright owner.
